text:
Then I walked into medicine.

And I assumed it would work the same way. You learn the craft. You find your master. You put in the reps. You get good.

But medicine is not built like that.

Medicine is built on a different assumption. That if you know enough, you will perform enough. That knowledge and execution are the same thing. That a pathway memorized is a pathway walked.

I believed that for longer than I should have.

The first time a real patient was in front of me, I found out it was not true.
preview: Then I walked into medicine.  And I assumed it would work the same way. You learn the craft. You find your master. You put in the reps. You get good.
